finding.contract_claim: Symbol `get_dead_code_tools` is declared but appears unused — no references were found outside its own declaration.
finding.observed_behavior: No callers or importers of `get_dead_code_tools` were found in the fixture project; consumer modules import the get_*_tool_definitions() variants instead, so this wrapper is never invoked even though the TOOL constant it returns is alive.

finding.triage_reasoning: Reality: Zero references to `get_dead_code_tools` in the 6-file sweep. The same pattern as Claim 0: the raw-dict variant was replaced by `get_dead_code_tool_definitions()`. The `get_*_tool_definitions()` functions are the live API; the `get_*_tools()` wrappers are orphaned. Significance: Dead code adds noise and can mislead future contributors. Actionability: Delete the function at L391-393.
